name: add-effect
tagline: Step-by-step guide for adding a new WebGL2/2D effect to the @remotion/effects package, including code, docs, demos, and tests.
verdict: A precise, highly actionable internal contributor guide for the Remotion monorepo. Commands, file paths, and naming conventions are concrete and consistent. Weak spots: no explicit failure handling if build/test/render steps fail, no version/changelog signal, and re-running for the same effect name risks duplicate registry entries (exports, sidebar, TOC).

highlights.summary: Step 10 build/test/render steps ('bun test', 'bunx turbo make', 'bunx remotion still') have no stated behavior if any command fails.
Registering exports/sidebar/TOC entries (steps 3, 5) has no existence check, so re-running for the same effect name would duplicate entries.
Relies on bun/bunx/turbo/oxfmt tooling throughout with no availability check or install guidance.
